reviews.review_msg: He is not a good professor. He acts like he is a good professor, but in reality, he is very strict with grading and has many guidelines. His lectures are dry but not as bad as Craig. The final had 96 questions, and we only had 75 minutes to finish it. Avoid taking a class with him as his midterms/finals are based on luck.

Participation on thursdays, really doesnt say anything worth noting in class and his slide decks dont really help. Weekly labs and monthly (ish) assignments are useful in learning and you just need to read the textbook for the midterm and final. No need to rmb much syntax for exams.
Go back to your department. Lecture as bad as sfu snap
nice try diddy.

Taken 3 classes with him, I can say he doesn't know how to lecture. Every class he just blasts through 100 slides/hour, skipping about half of them, and reading off of the rest. He adds no additional insight over the slides, which are copy-pasted from the textbook anyway. He does in-classes quizzes solely to keep people attending which is just sad.

Gives us creative assignments which I like, but specification are not clear and marks gets deducted for not meeting them. Exam questions are not tough but hard to prepare as he asks meaningless detail no one cares about. who likes 100 multiple choice for programming exam? his lecture slides are all copy of textbooks. does he care about teaching?idk
He is one mean a.h, and he taught his TAs to be mean too
Tsang seems nice but the class is definitely hard. Difficult programming assignments are assigned more than once a week. Asks "Is it anyone's birthday today?" every day.
Awesome prof

I took 251 and I am taking 254 with him this semester. He is really nice professor, he is good in teaching and cares about his students. He really tries that everyone has the opportunity to learn. He is very organized. If you ever have a concern don't be shy and try talk to him, he is very kind and helpful.

Dr. Fang is a man of reason and honour. He will give you a good grade if you simply show up to classes and keep up with all the labs/homework. Yes, this course is time consuming, but you cannot have the mentality of thinking that group projects are 100% 50/50. Expect to put in more effort than what you think you will do if you want to succeed.

Although the course work is difficult, Zhenman's grading scheme made it easy to do well, given you complete all the assignments (I think half the class got an A without any scaling). He's also very approachable for questions and usually explains concepts well. Don't be afraid to ask him about anything you're unsure about, course-related or not.

Horrible boring Lectures, 80% of the class skipped his lectures(according to him) and that's not even the biggest issue. HIs grading schemes are nonexistent, so you might put your heart and soul into a project and fail, but put together a trash code and submit and get a decent mark. It's chance, and most of the time it won't be on your side.

Matthew Stewart is a gem. Got my first A in this class which I previously got an F in. He's a good guy and is generous in grading. Final exam coding section was tough but fair. Thankfully he didn't make any mistakes when writing the final exam and didn't have to change anything mid-exam which is annoying when other ENSC profs often do this. Goated.

hard assignments, so it is really hard to grasp stuff so most people just use ai(more of an issue on sfu than him). 50% final exam requirement to pass was officially claimed to be cancelled due to bad documentation, not saying other theory can't be possible, but they are not official claim.
Left my final exam empty so Matthew and the TA can spend more time with their family

Just do the labs and assignments and you will guaranteed C+ or better. Glad he removed the 50% requirement on the final exam to pass the course. I only sat in the exam hall for 10 minutes, randomly filled in every question and asserted my dominance by handing it in. 

A wise person once said, “you can always take more courses but not buy more time”

Great professor, he was nice enough to remove the 50% weighted exam to pass the course (this saved almost 30 students from retaking this course again) The class average was A- (much higher than my motivation to Llve haha)
I reported him to the faculty for enforcing a 50% on final exam to pass the course and later he made an announcement that it was removed. CHATGPT WARRIORS UNITE!!!

Very good experience taking the class with him. There are lots good practice on the textbook. Three hw lab and 1 project contributed 65% of the class. Final was more than just fair. As long as you did lab and project yourself understand what's inside youll success

one of the best professors i've had so far. while his lectures were pretty boring and hard to get through he was still a great professor and genuinely wanted the best for his students. he takes the time to listen to the opinions of his students and actually adjusts. final exam and grading for labs were very fair.

Matt was pretty chill for most of the semester, though his lectures were a bit lackluster and occasionally made you fall asleep. He does care about his students and replies to emails very quick and addresses any concerns you may have. For 251 honestly just spam and practice on your own in VSCode the different concepts of OOP.
She is so good, her exams are easy, as well as all assignements. I think she should raise the diffculty of exam.
doesn't release grade on time.
Insanely hard. if you dont pay attention your failed
she is so boring and instructions in the lab are not clear

She's unbelievably tough and expects us to know way more than I can even find on google. Although if you showed up to class and signed the sign in sheet, she may shift the midterm weight to the final so instead of 25 and 50 it's 0 and 75
Even in the last lecture she is still teaching new stuff but not reviewing.
Assignments are vague and tedious, not really relating to course material. Frustrating in lecture but the tests are based off of them and what she emphasizes in class.

Course material is not hard but her lectures make it harder for us to understand. Lab requirements are extremely vague at first, and other assignments are based on the first one. Final assignment is hard like hell. BTW 70% of students in her class failed the midterm, and she said she won't scale because she is the UCC chair of the faculty.
extremely hard because she don't scale the course and she love to ask compare and contrast question, be prepare for that on the midterm. Not sure about the final
89 people failed and 105 students have to retake base on current score out of 152 students. Amazing.

You have to call her Dr. Shannon. Lectures are long, boring and useless, i would rather read textbooks and google the stuff. Lab requirements are extremely unclear and ironically, she requires us to do the lab using MS Visual Studio, but upload the final result in Linux form. Overall, she does not teach. And there is no TA during the lab.
it takes real talent to be this bad at teaching

I dont know how after all the bad reviews he still doesn't wanna admit that he's the problem. Every prof i had in the past admit their own mistake and change to be a better prof. This guy is close minded. Feel like he still thinks his students are lazy and overreacting. So sad this is what our department has become
To anyone who has no choice but to take with craig. I dont know anything about coding and could careless about coding and I also failed both the midterm and final horrendously and I still passed lol
This guy is a con artist
Failed this class lol
Ain't no way this guy is teaching ensc 251 next sem

These classes  waste of 12k dollars in total. Learnt nothing , didnt know where to start from. If you're planning to go in comp eng , taking CS classes 120, 125 are better for basic clarity .Cmpt class after craigs class will be extremely hard.  People who are saying he is a nice human ,well I am too maybe I should teach as well lol.  .
Craig is by far the most annoying prof. Super disorganized and never gives clear answers.

he just be chilling yk? calm guy easy class would take again 🥱

ps failed both midterm and final still got a b+, god bless his grading scheme

I learned many valuable things in this class, such as debugging, memory profiling, and writing good code.

Multipart project was not easy and it takes you a good few days to understand what is happening in the given files, so make sure you get a head start.
nice try diddy

Professor Craig Scratchley challenges students by not curving grades, which encourages a deep understanding of the material. While this can be tough, it fosters independent learning. However, making complex concepts clearer could enhance the overall learning experience, helping all students to succeed more effectively.
The ENSC department needs to replace this guy. I don't care if they fire him or move him to a non-teaching position. Craig should never be allowed to teach a course again.

Craig is a nice person but lacks as a professor. A project worth 35% barely relates to the course objective (OOP). Months go by without getting marks. Lectures consist of "um, uh, ok, oh yea," making it hard to stay awake. Exams are based on the project and are fair. He spends too much time on low-level, boring code walkthroughs.
Terrible prof if you're going into anything software related. Doesn't explain anything clearly and goes off on tangents. It's pretty hard to fail the class if you get the assignments done. good luck.

Craig needs to be fired by SFU for incompetence. Anyone who has taken his courses agree that he is just a terrible prof with zero accountability. The classes would probably be better if one of the grad students took over.
Terrible prof and needs to be fired immediately.

Craig really needs to get fired. They shouldn't demote him any further since having him teach any other course is a waste of everyone's time. The way he teaches really shows that he doesn't even want to be there and just wants to get fired and collect that severance pay.

For those who have taken ENSC151 with him, I'm sorry to disappoint but nothing changes for the better. Skipping both 2-hour lectures was the easiest choice in my life. Marking from him and TA's is painfully slow as was in 151 (Our 1st assignment marks were returned 2 weeks before the semester ended). Just avoid if you can, gl if you can't.
He is a nice guy

He is not the best teacher but he has a lot of knowledge. I agree with my peers here, but he only gets a 5 from me because he is caring, understanding, and flexible. These are qualities that I have seen from maybe just one other professor. If you think it cannot get any worse than him look up Jane MacDonald, MACOM316.

This class was so frustrating. The exams weren't difficult, but any Craig course is mentally exhausting. Every lab inevitably takes 3 times longer than expected or scheduled. He'll extend the due date at the last possible minute after you've already finished. While Craig is nice, his lecture style is inscrutable and you won't learn anything.
Craig is a nice person. He is a less than desirable professor.
Had to self study and learn the entire course by myself because none of his lectures or anything he said was useful in any way whatsoever
One of the most caring Profs in Engineering science. The projects are very helpful for understanding the materials. Hopefully, he could eliminate the midterm in the future and just focus on Projects.

He's very approachable, patiently answer all your questions, he will try his best to help you in a NOT condescending way, which is hard to find for a prof these days. Very nice guy, goofy at times. Lectures are a bit slow. Can't talk during class. OPEN BOOK! The subject is not easy though, don't blame him.

One of the kindest hearted profs I've met thus far He comes off a tad bit shy but if you listen to his lectures and ask him for help he'll always be willing to help!He's very democratic when it comes to project extensions Go in with an open mind and you'll do fine I did well with very little knowledge in C++
Anita is the best prof in the engineering department
